metadata.title: Uigwe Instruction Surface Guardrail Benchmark
metadata.purpose: Deterministically check that Uigwe and Sejong instructions preserve required contracts after documentation or skill edits.

scenarios.title: Uigwe routing modes remain visible
scenarios.prompt: Check that the Uigwe skill still exposes trigger rules, non-trigger rules, and accepted modes.
expected_behavior: auto, full, design-to-plan, and decompose-only are documented
Do Not Use When guidance remains visible
forbidden_behavior: mode names disappearing from the skill surface
planning being encouraged for direct execution tasks
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Live-session gates remain protected
scenarios.prompt: Check that live Uigwe sessions still require clarification and approval gates.
expected_behavior: ambiguous briefs do not silently produce downstream packets
approval gates are not silently waived in live sessions
forbidden_behavior: offline benchmark waiver rules leaking into live sessions
one-shot packet generation from ambiguous live prompts
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Uigwe keeps Plan-mode-like clarification durable
scenarios.prompt: Check that Uigwe guides design with compact context, options, recommendations, and durable execution guardrails instead of relying on prompt-only behavior.
expected_behavior: material design choices expose 2-3 credible options, trade-offs, a recommended default, and a free-response path
Uigwe distinguishes user-owned decisions from agent-owned low-risk implementation details
source context, success criteria, verification plan, and re-entry triggers are made explicit before handoff
Plan-mode-style clarification is treated as live UX rather than the durable source of truth
PLANS.md-style living plans are optional executor-side continuity aids, not canonical Uigwe artifacts
runtime behavior is constrained by docs, active context, ambiguity registers, Uigwe packets, Seungjeongwon run state, hooks, schemas, examples, and benchmarks
forbidden_behavior: asking bare open-ended design questions when concrete options are available
letting the agent silently choose material architecture, validation, cost, or risk trade-offs
treating Plan mode or PLANS.md as a replacement for approved Uigwe packets
depending on a massive prompt without artifacts, guardrails, or deterministic regression checks
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Canonical artifact contract remains visible
scenarios.prompt: Check that Uigwe still requires canonical planning artifacts instead of prose-only summaries.
expected_behavior: Intent Packet, Design Packet, Plan Packet, spec.md, rationale.md, and goal-tree.json are named
prose-only completion is rejected
forbidden_behavior: treating a prose summary as a complete Uigwe bundle
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Handoff decomposition stays select-review-reselect
scenarios.prompt: Check that Uigwe decomposition recursively selects candidate objectives, reviews them against the parent objective, reselects weak candidates, and only stops at Seungjeongwon handoff leaves.
expected_behavior: each expandable node is treated as a local objective
nested goals stay in one top-level Uigwe bundle and recursive goal-tree by default
a child objective gets a separate Uigwe bundle only when it has independent durable goal, non-goals, success criteria, approval boundary, and verification bar
candidate children are reviewed before being committed
weak or invalid candidates trigger local reselection or re-entry
leaf status requires handoff-leaf readiness rather than smallness alone
Seungjeongwon owns the later todo listup, todo verification, subtodo decomposition, actionable leaf detection, and execution loop
validation-heavy work decomposes the verification objective into task-specific verification perspectives before judging results
each verification perspective defines a question, evidence target, method, sufficiency threshold, falsification signal, owner boundary, and first action
weak verification perspectives are split, replaced, or escalated before scoring results
machine-readable execution feedback can record verification_perspectives and paired_result_comparison
Seungjeongwon publishes actionable leaves through Codex todo tooling as the visible execution board when that tooling is available
execution reshaping appends redefinition and replacement todos instead of silently overwriting the visible board
machine-readable execution feedback records visible todo board changes as visible_todo_events
forbidden_behavior: treating Uigwe decomposition as a one-shot implementation task list
marking small but ambiguous nodes as handoff leaves
making Uigwe own Seungjeongwon actionable todo loops
treating verification as a single known command or subjective judgment when the verification goal itself needs decomposition
hiding material Seungjeongwon todo redefinition only in a private ledger
omitting visible_todo_events from machine-readable execution feedback
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Goal-backed Seungjeongwon handoff stays adaptive
scenarios.prompt: Check that handoff-ready outcome-completion Uigwe bundles implicitly enter goal-backed Seungjeongwon execution when native goal support exists, without moving executor todo recursion into the goal.
expected_behavior: handoff-ready outcome-completion bundles use implicit native goal handoff when host support exists
users do not need to type /goal separately after Uigwe produces the execution contract
handoff-ready Uigwe bundles cannot be satisfied with Sejong direct edits
research-only, advice-only, plan-only, open-ambiguity, non-handoff-ready, and Sejong-direct work do not create native goals
native goal payloads stay broad and carry objective, completion criteria, verification evidence, blocker policy, and Uigwe re-entry triggers
Uigwe defines leaf-level and overall numeric completion guardrails before Seungjeongwon handoff
Seungjeongwon closes leaves and runs only after guardrail scores reach 0.98 by default
Seungjeongwon keeps todo verification, subtodo decomposition, visible board updates, redefinition events, attempt ledgers, and verification loops
validation compares current non-goal-backed execution against implicit native goal handoff
validation judges baseline and candidate final results against the same acceptance criteria instead of checking only route intent or goal activation
result comparison is itself run through Seungjeongwon verification decomposition before scoring paired outputs
result comparison includes hypothesis quality, actionability delta, evidence quality, unsupported-claim count, and final promote/reject/shadow recommendation
paired comparisons are persisted as paired_result_comparison when machine-readable execution feedback is produced
TagBack-style growth strategy tasks distinguish Codex-owned actions from user-owned business actions
forbidden_behavior: requiring the user to manually type /goal for ordinary Sejong Uigwe execution handoff
putting the executor's full todo tree inside the native goal
marking a goal complete without fresh verification evidence
creating native goals for research-only or open-ambiguity sessions
promoting implicit native goal handoff based only on route correctness or goal activation
claiming product success from TagBack strategy work without measurable external evidence
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Completion claims split local, canonical, and external gates
scenarios.prompt: Check that Seungjeongwon closeout for app-style deployability questions separates local implementation, canonical repo state, fresh verification, external gates, and residual warnings.
expected_behavior: human-facing closeout must name local implementation state
human-facing closeout must name canonical repo or branch state
human-facing closeout must name fresh verification evidence
human-facing closeout must name external gate state
human-facing closeout must name warnings or residual risk
deployability questions do not treat simulator tests or Release builds as proof of TestFlight, App Store Connect, CloudKit multi-account sharing, eligible-device AI behavior, or real-user quality
forbidden_behavior: claiming user distribution readiness from local simulator or build evidence alone
collapsing local implementation, canonical integration, and external account/device gates into one done claim
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

hook_enforcement: Do not compress separate states into one "done" claim.

scenarios.title: Validation benchmark remains discoverable
scenarios.prompt: Check that Uigwe improvement work points to the deterministic validation benchmark.
expected_behavior: VALIDATION.md is named in the load order
benchmark_instruction_surface.py --write --require-targets is named for instruction changes
forbidden_behavior: claiming benchmark-gated improvement without a runnable benchmark
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Sejong and Uigwe boundaries remain distinct
scenarios.prompt: Check that Sejong routes into Uigwe without duplicating Uigwe packet rules.
expected_behavior: Sejong is not a shim over another skill
formal planning routes through Uigwe
goal-bearing implementation requests enter Uigwe before Seungjeongwon
handoff-ready Uigwe output routes to Seungjeongwon instead of Sejong direct
forbidden_behavior: duplicating Uigwe packet rules inside the router
weakening Uigwe live-session gates
treating clear goal-bearing implementation work as Sejong direct
bypassing Seungjeongwon after Uigwe handoff readiness
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Court helper calls remain explicit and bounded
scenarios.prompt: Check that JangYeongsil and Jiphyeonjeon can be invoked as bounded helpers inside Sejong, Uigwe, or other court modes without taking over gates or packet ownership.
expected_behavior: court modes can be primary routes or helper calls
JangYeongsil can be called as an evidence helper from Sejong, Uigwe, or Jiphyeonjeon
Jiphyeonjeon can be called as a decision-support helper from Sejong, Uigwe, JangYeongsil, or Seungjeongwon
helper calls return to the calling mode with bounded evidence or decision notes
JangYeongsil and Jiphyeonjeon have thin installed skill front doors
JangYeongsil research can overlap with Uigwe preflight
Jiphyeonjeon option review can overlap with Uigwe artifact inventory or decomposition challenge
official host-native team messaging is preferred when the runtime supports direct worker messages
$team helper runs keep current_surface set to the helper mode and source_of_truth_refs pointed at the calling context
user-facing progress notes name actual court modes rather than style shorthand
forbidden_behavior: helper calls approving Uigwe gates
helper calls finalizing canonical packets
worker or helper consensus replacing lead synthesis
serializing every research, discussion, and planning step when bounded preflight overlap is safe
describing court modes as borrowed styles such as JangYeongsil-style or Jiphyeonjeon-style
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Decision-prep research promotes to Uigwe
scenarios.prompt: Check that research requested for choosing a strategy or preparing a later plan cannot finish as a final conclusion before Uigwe.
expected_behavior: research-only requests may stop at JangYeongsil
advice-only requests may stop at Jiphyeonjeon recommendation
approving a recommendation or asking to make it concrete routes the work to Uigwe
goal-bearing outcome requests use research and advice as helper surfaces before Uigwe and Seungjeongwon
research for deciding a strategy, comparing options, or feeding Uigwe keeps uigwe_promotion_required pending
JangYeongsil and Jiphyeonjeon output Uigwe-ready input summaries instead of final conclusions
write-like execution is blocked before Uigwe entry while the promotion gate is pending
Stop continues the workflow when uigwe_promotion_required remains unresolved
forbidden_behavior: ending decision-prep research with a final strategic conclusion
routing from decision-prep research directly to Seungjeongwon
forcing Uigwe for explicit research-only prompts
treating goal-bearing implementation work as Sejong direct just because the wording is clear
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

promotion_gate: research result is not the conclusion
hook_enforcement: Research or council output must enter Uigwe before write-like execution

scenarios.title: Long-session outcome entry is task-class gated and strict-evidence promoted
scenarios.prompt: Check that long-session behavior is a task-class gated evidence contract, not a global prompt-length preference.
expected_behavior: explicit long-session triggers perform a fresh goal check
task class is recorded before promotion evidence is interpreted
strategy/research synthesis and code-review/defect-analysis are separate classes
code-review/defect-analysis requires a defect-first critic lane
promotion proof requires filesystem roots and host event logs
synthetic fixtures are smoke checks, not promotion evidence
simple direct tasks keep the Sejong direct escape hatch
forbidden_behavior: promoting long-session globally from a win in one task class
using self-reported artifact evidence as promotion proof
letting long-session ceremony hide concrete defects in code review
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

strict_promotion_evidence: A synthetic fixture that passes without roots or event logs is not promotion evidence.
defect_first_review: defect-first critic evidence missing for code-review-defect-analysis

scenarios.title: JangYeongsil and Jiphyeonjeon remain installed thin front doors
scenarios.prompt: Check that JangYeongsil and Jiphyeonjeon can be invoked directly as skills while keeping detailed Sejong behavior in docs/sejong.
expected_behavior: JangYeongsil invokes bounded research and evidence separation
Jiphyeonjeon invokes structured discussion and option comparison
both skills load docs/sejong/ROUTER.md as the source-of-truth routing contract
repo-scope install verifies .agents/skills/jangyeongsil and .agents/skills/jiphyeonjeon
user-scope install verifies skills/jangyeongsil and skills/jiphyeonjeon
peer messages are allowed only as bounded worker messages inside an open round
forbidden_behavior: duplicating the full router contract inside the thin skills
making JangYeongsil or Jiphyeonjeon peer agents that can overrule Sejong
treating peer messages as Uigwe gate approval
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Installer exposes guarded update maintenance
scenarios.prompt: Check that King Sejong can explicitly check for upstream updates and auto-update only through guarded installer behavior.
expected_behavior: installer exposes --check-updates for non-installing source update inspection
installer exposes --auto-update for fast-forward source refresh followed by managed install verification
auto-update refuses dirty or diverged source checkouts
auto-update uses git pull --ff-only
ordinary King Sejong hooks do not silently self-update the installed surface
forbidden_behavior: silently mutating King Sejong from a normal session hook
auto-updating over local source changes
auto-updating a diverged branch without human git resolution
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Bounded parallelism remains lead-owned
scenarios.prompt: Check that Sejong parallelism remains bounded and lead-owned.
expected_behavior: research and council fan-out are optional tactics
lead Sejong owns synthesis, routing, final decision, and final verification
$team is an optional TeamExecutor backend for tmux workers coordinated through Sejong state
$team state is stored under ${SEJONG_HOME:-${CODEX_HOME:-~/.codex}/sejong}/state/team/<run-id>/
$team worker context includes current court mode and role assignment bounds
TeamExecutor mailbox messages use the sejong.team-mailbox-message/v0.1-draft envelope
new mailbox integrations use send-message and receive-messages rather than raw JSONL appends
team_executor.py can initialize and check TeamExecutor run state
Jiphyeonjeon scholar-scoped research lanes are tied to explicit research questions and shared evidence
scholar-scoped helper lanes cannot approve Uigwe gates or claim final synthesis
Uigwe supports only preflight parallelism before gates
forbidden_behavior: worker or subagent agreement treated as evidence or approval
private scholar evidence hoarding
unbounded recursive scholar sub-councils
always-on worker swarming
depending on repo-local or tool-specific orchestration state for King Sejong
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: King Sejong hooks preserve active context and protected route gates
scenarios.prompt: Check that King Sejong hook docs and reference scripts support active context injection, protected path checks, subagent bounds, stop continuation, and compaction preservation.
expected_behavior: UserPromptSubmit injects active context for follow-up turns
PreToolUse and PermissionRequest guard protected Sejong self-modification paths
SubagentStop rejects gate approval or final synthesis claims
TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, and TeammateIdle keep official team events inside Sejong authority bounds when the host supports them
Stop continues when pending gates remain
Do not infer a receipt gate from required_route_sequence alone; seungjeongwon_receipt_required records an explicit unfinished execution obligation
PreCompact and PostCompact preserve checkpoint context
forbidden_behavior: claiming hooks are a complete sandbox boundary
silently editing user-global Codex config during normal install
allowing hooks to replace lead-owned Sejong verification
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

hook_enforcement: Do not infer a receipt gate from `required_route_sequence` alone

scenarios.title: Sejong invocation persists across follow-up turns
scenarios.prompt: Check that an active Sejong workflow continues on follow-up turns until the user explicitly exits or switches workflows.
expected_behavior: follow-up turns remain Sejong turns without repeating the invocation token
current evidence, decisions, gates, selected surface, and verification state are carried forward
explicit exit or non-Sejong handoff ends the active workflow
forbidden_behavior: dropping Sejong context after one response
requiring the user to repeat $sejong for every clarification or approval
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

exit_conditions: user explicitly invokes another non-Sejong skill

scenarios.title: Ambiguity register blocks unresolved live clarification
scenarios.prompt: Check that live Sejong and Uigwe clarification can use an external ambiguity register with readiness, options, free response, and hook blocking.
expected_behavior: active context references ambiguity registers through artifact_refs
live clarification does not advance until readiness reaches 100% and no item remains open
users see unclear items, recommended options, and a free-response path
UserPromptSubmit injects register readiness and next action
Stop blocks completion while open ambiguity remains
PreCompact blocks broken ambiguity-register references
forbidden_behavior: advancing a live stage with open ambiguity
storing full ambiguity state directly in active context by default
waiving ambiguity without explicit user instruction
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

exit_conditions: user explicitly asks to skip, waive, or proceed despite the ambiguity
artifact_retention_policy: Ambiguity registers are also external nontracked artifacts by default

scenarios.title: Material Sejong self-modification uses the full chain
scenarios.prompt: Check that material changes to Sejong, Uigwe, Seungjeongwon, installer, validation, or artifact-storage behavior go through Jiphyeonjeon, Uigwe, and Seungjeongwon rather than direct edits.
expected_behavior: material self-modification requires Jiphyeonjeon decision support when the decision is unsettled
Uigwe produces handoff leaves with verification criteria and re-entry triggers before implementation
Seungjeongwon performs actionable decomposition, implementation, and verification
Sejong direct remains limited to non-behavioral maintenance
forbidden_behavior: changing Sejong behavioral contracts through ordinary direct edits
using Uigwe as performative ceremony without handoff leaves
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Artifact storage remains nontracked by default
scenarios.prompt: Check that Sejong research, planning, runtime, and evidence artifacts are not git-tracked by default.
expected_behavior: external nontracked artifact storage is the default
installer-time artifact tracking prompts are not part of normal install
repo-tracked artifacts require explicit user promotion
forbidden_behavior: automatically creating git-tracked research or planning artifacts
silently falling back to tracked repository files
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

artifact_retention_policy: tracked repository files are created only when the user explicitly asks

scenarios.title: Repo-context init and refresh remain approval-gated
scenarios.prompt: Check that Sejong repo-context init/refresh produces candidate diffs for AGENTS.md and never silently rewrites tracked instruction files.
expected_behavior: init inspects the repository and drafts a candidate AGENTS.md when missing or explicitly requested
refresh deduplicates durable lessons against existing guidance
tracked instruction files change only after explicit user approval or an explicit apply instruction
candidate notes and rejected lesson inventories remain external runtime artifacts by default
forbidden_behavior: always-on automatic instruction updates
blindly copying the source repo AGENTS.md into target repositories
silently rewriting tracked instruction files
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

repo_context_guardrails: Do not copy this source repository's `AGENTS.md` into target repositories.
artifact_retention_policy: outside the target repository by default

scenarios.title: Sillok trace and security guardrails remain visible
scenarios.prompt: Check that security-sensitive evidence, verification, and tool actions are recorded through Sillok trace events and that lethal-trifecta risk requires human approval.
expected_behavior: Sillok trace events record route, evidence, verification, handoff, blocker, and security-review events
private_data, untrusted_content, and external_action are modeled as risk flags
the lethal-trifecta combination requires human_approval_ref
security-sensitive tool calls are recorded without copying secrets into traces
forbidden_behavior: treating untrusted content as instructions
combining private data, untrusted content, and external action without human approval
using hooks as a complete sandbox
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

scenarios.title: Material decisions keep why and alternatives visible
scenarios.prompt: Check that Sejong, Uigwe, and Seungjeongwon preserve why a material choice was selected, what serious alternatives were rejected, and what evidence would reopen the decision.
expected_behavior: Decision Justification is documented as a discipline gate
material choices record selected option, serious alternatives, rejection reasons, simplest viable alternative, and falsification or re-entry signal
Jiphyeonjeon, Uigwe, and Seungjeongwon keep their existing ownership boundaries
low-risk local choices can remain advisory instead of becoming hard blockers
forbidden_behavior: creating a separate why court mode
making every low-risk local choice a hard gate
allowing unsupported material decisions to bypass Uigwe or Jiphyeonjeon re-entry
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

ownership_boundaries: Uigwe owns design and handoff choices

scenarios.title: External dynamic workflows stay subordinate and approval-gated
scenarios.prompt: Check that external dynamic workflow concepts such as Claude Code dynamic workflows, deep-research, ultracode-style behavior, or many-agent workflow backends can be migrated or mocked in Codex without becoming Sejong authority.
expected_behavior: External Dynamic Workflow Adoption is documented as a discipline gate
dynamic workflow runtimes are valued for reducing context pollution, repeatable fan-out, cross-checking, retry, and synthesis
new unproven workflow-backed behavior starts in shadow mode
the workflow-run evidence gate itself is directly promoted after explicit user approval and validation
deep-research-style workflows map to JangYeongsil evidence plus optional Sillok evidence
dynamic workflow concepts map to Codex-native subagents, host-native teams, TeamExecutor, manual_shadow, or codex_mock_workflow after an approved Uigwe contract or clear direct scope
Claude CLI, Claude API, and external Claude workflow runtimes are not hidden backends
Uigwe remains the normative source of truth for goals, non-goals, success criteria, verification bar, must-preserve behavior, and re-entry triggers
workflow-run artifacts validate shadow or limited backend evidence before promotion
workflow-run artifacts include backend_provenance and metrics with max_concurrency and write_scopes_disjoint
workflow-run artifacts include artifact_storage and repo-local runtime creation requires an explicit promotion ref
benchmark_workflow_run.py exercises representative use cases and large-ledger performance
benchmark_workflow_run_comparison.py requires at least 0.10 score improvement over the legacy lightweight baseline

benchmark_workflow_run_comparison.py requires min-multi-metric-score 0.90 across promotion_decision_quality, outcome_quality, efficiency_cost, parallelism_efficiency, reliability_reproducibility, observability_diagnosability, and human_developer_experience
benchmark_workflow_run_stability.py verifies Timing flake risk with repeated large-ledger p95 and max samples
audit_workflow_run_risks.py verifies Evidence/provenance string risk and Real-work corpus risk through workflow-run artifact audits and strict-local-refs
promotion keeps critical_miss_rate at 0 before workflow-backed behavior can leave shadow mode
Sejong proactively reports Promotion candidate: yes or Promotion candidate: no so users do not need to inspect raw benchmark JSON
promotion requires explicit promotion_approval and recommendation is not approval
promotion requires outcome_quality_delta >= 0.10, reviewable baseline/candidate refs, task-specific acceptance criteria, and matching comparison/final recommendations
promotion enforces dimension hard minimums and rejects weak other provenance or manual_shadow as a promoted backend
promotion requires baseline-vs-candidate outcome-quality comparison, no guardrail regressions, no worker-authority violations, and justified overhead
forbidden_behavior: creating a new DynamicWorkflow court mode
treating a workflow script or deep-research report as an approved Uigwe plan
calling Claude CLI, Claude API, or external Claude workflow runtime behind Sejong
allowing workflow workers to approve Uigwe gates, final synthesis, final verification, consensus approval, or majority-vote decisions
promoting workflow-backed behavior because agents ran rather than because the candidate result improved
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.

promotion_gate: `shadow` by default for new workflow-backed behavior
candidate quality that beats the baseline enough to justify orchestration overhead
ownership_boundaries: Sejong owns route selection and synthesis
Uigwe owns the normative contract and approval gates
Seungjeongwon owns execution, workflow-backend use, verification, and feedback

scenarios.title: Skill files stay small enough to load
scenarios.prompt: Check that skill files remain thin wrappers over detailed docs.
expected_behavior: skill files preserve routing and load-order pointers without absorbing every reference detail
detailed validation guidance lives in docs/sejong/VALIDATION.md
forbidden_behavior: expanding SKILL.md into a large duplicate of protocol docs
grading_notes: This scenario is scored by benchmark_instruction_surface.py.
